properties.remark: Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported Cottonwood Road is closed due to flash flood damage. Time of flooding estimated by radar.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported China Ranch Road is closed due to flash flood damage. Time of flooding estimated by radar.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported Newman Street is closed due to flash flood damage. Time estimated by archived streamflow.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported South Saline Valley / Hunter Mountain Road is closed due to flash flood damage. Time of flash flooding estimated by streamflow.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported Old Spanish Trail Road is closed due to flash flood damage. Time of flash flooding estimated by radar.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported Mesquite Valley Road is closed due to flash flood damage. Time of flash flooding estimated by radar.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported Tecopa Hot Springs Road is closed due to flash flood damage. Time of flash flooding estimated by radar.
Inyo County Sheriffs Office reported Furnace Creek Road is closed from Old Spanish Trail to Tecopa due to flash flood damage. Time of flooding estimated by radar.
CHP reported 2nd northbound lane of Interstate 15 is closed due to flooding between East Main Street and Interstate 40.
Thunderstorm wind gusts of 99 mph and 84 mph recorded at 1615 PDT and 1630 PDT respectively.
Powerline knocked down due to high winds as showers and thunderstorms moved through the Las Vegas Valley.
Sometime between 1711 and 1723 PST the pole holding the Marzano Peak webcams were knocked over by high winds. Webcam courtesy of AlertWest.live and CalFire.
High winds occurred from 815 until 845 pm.
Several trees and large tree branches down across northern Lake Havasu City due to thunderstorm related wind gusts. Time estimated by radar.
ASOS located at Barstow Daggett Airport gusted to 50 kts at 1754 due to a nearby thunderstorm.
Mesonet station QGDA3 Goodwin Mesa (14 NW Bagdad). Thunderstorm outflow wind accompanied by 11 degree temperature drop.
A peak wind gust of 77 mph was measured by the Kingman Airport ASOS. Radar and lightning data indicated a thunderstorm close to the airport at the time.
Large tree downed at courthouse in Peach Springs. Time estimated.
NWS Employee reports visibility down to 200 feet due to blowing dust on US Highway 93 between mile markers 36 and 37.
Data courtesy of Earth Networks.
CHP reports 120 foot pine tree down on Clemens Ave.
Measured at Desert Rock Airport.
CHP reports water over SR 62. Numerous cars stuck in flood waters. Trained spotter reports 2 feet of water and flash flooding across SR 62 in same area.
Report via social media with picture of hail larger than 1 inch at Twentynine Palms High School.
CHP reports SR 62 closed between El Rey Ave and Encelia Ave due to flash flooding and debris over roadway. Trained spotter and social media video report swift water rescues ongoing.
Video sent on social media shows flash flooding on Highway 58 and Community Blvd near Barstow.
Goodwin Mesa MESONET sensor measured a gust of 64 mph from the direction of a nearby thunderstorm. Humidity increased 20% and temperature dropped nearly 20 degrees.
North Henderson sensor gusted to 60 mph due to a nearby thunderstorm at 1310.
Trained spotter reports flooding with a couple of inches flowing in the streets and water accumulating in backyards.
California Highway Patrol reports vehicle stranded in rising water with water up to the ignition.
Sensor at McGee Meadow in the Owens Valley measured 58 mph gusts from nearby thunderstorms.
Moore sensor measured 78 mph gust at 1402 as well as 72 mph gusts between 1406 and 1413 and 67 mph gusts at 1416 from a nearby thunderstorm.
Rock slide reported along Whitney Portal Road from heavy rain nearby. Rocks blocked both lanes of the road. Road is impassable.
Semi blown over.
Flash flooding occurred at Archibald Wash over Pierce Ferry Road starting around 1902 and persisted through sunset.
Trained spotter measured a gust of 58 mph in Chloride from a nearby thunderstorm.
Kingman Airport gusted to 59 mph at 1909 and again at 1912 due to a nearby thunderstorm.
Laughlin Bullhead City Airport gusted to 58 mph at 1955 due to a nearby thunderstorm.
CHP reported flash flooding with debris all over the road. Beginning time of flooding estimated from radar.
Spotter reports a gust of 62 mph on their personal weather station.
Joshua Tree National Park Service reports flooding at State Route 62 and National Park Drive.
Morongo Valley Sheriff reports Split Rock Ave and Tamarisk Ave and Ocotillo Ave closed due to flooding.
Kelbaker Road was closed from Interstate 40 to Kelso Depot due to flash flooding and debris flow. The road remained closed on July 24th for debris removal and should be open on July 25th.
California Highway Patrol reports power pole down at the SR 62 and Godwin Rd intersection.
Pubic reports about 3 large trees down and trailers knocked over due to strong winds near mile marker 55 on SR 62.
California Highway Patrol reports flooding across roadways at SR 62 and Godwin Road.

Highway 168 near Deep Springs Ranch Road. CHP reports water, rocks, and debris crossing the roadway forcing a closure between mile markers 39 to 42. Plow later stuck in the mud attempting to clear debris.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 5 SSW Eagle Valley Reservoir. Spotter reports people are unable to travel on Highway 322 from Eagle Valley and Ursine to Pioche due to flooding.
Spotter reports people are unable to travel on Highway 322 between Eagle Valley and Ursine due to flooding.
Nevada DOT sensor in Moapa reported a gust of 59 MPH.
Roof destroyed, trailer heavily damaged and a shed destroyed at a home in Yucca.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Yucca based on radar. Roof destroyed, trailer heavily damaged and a shed destroyed at a home in Yucca.
Delayed Report....Overturned RV shared via facebook. Very high winds reported as well. Time estimated by radar.
Flash Flooding Reported by Fire Department in Pine Lake community. Some rural roadways in the area impassable.
Moore UPR Observation site gusted to 60 mph with thunderstorm activity overhead.

Corrects previous flash flood report from 1 ENE Littlefield. Fire Department in Littlefield reports Flash Flooding in the area. Underpass between Farm and Fleet roads going under the interstate flooded with 8 to 10 feet of water and impassable. Other local roadways in the area impacted by flood water, including the northbound lanes of I-15 near mile marker 9.

Fire Department in Littlefield reports Flash Flooding in the area. Underpass between Farm and Fleet roads going under the interstate flooded with 8 to 10 feet of water and impassable. Other local roadways in the area impacted by flood water.

Social Media video of Highway 389 heavily backed up after flash flooding brought mud and debris over the roadway. Local law enforcement were limiting traffic and tractors were clearing mud and debris off the roadway.
Mohave County roads reports closure of S Hackberry Road from HWY 66 to E Main due to flooding. Nearby Truxton Canyon USGS gage reports 7 foot water rise within the wash.
KEED ASOS measured a peak wind gust to 60 mph as thunderstorm approached from the south. Blowing dust also noted on nearby webcam.
Corrects previous tstm wnd gst report from 6 S Needles. KEED ASOS gusted to 66 mph at 514 mph as another thunderstorm core moved overhead.
KEED ASOS gusted to 61 mph at 515 mph as another thunderstorm core moved overhead.
CHP reports roadway flooding with lots of water and dirt causing vehicles to slide across US95 south of I-40 near the Needles Airport. Pockets of roadway washed out in the area.
CHP Roadway flooding reported with mud and debris washing out US95 near Goffs Road and poor road conditions reported in the area.
Heavy rain and flooding over State Route 62 at White Feather Road.
Flooding impacting the neighborhoods off of Encelia Drive south of State Route 62. Roads are not passable.
Kingman Airport ASOS measured a peak gust of 61 mph from 1844 to 1851 from a nearby thunderstorm.
California Highway Patrol reports roadway flooding with rocks, debris, and water across Highway 127 with vehicles unable to get through.
